active_arcs.title: Blocking Eli's Phone Contact with Olivia
active_arcs.description: Three documented blocking mechanisms: (1) DND infrastructure at 8:30pm — unilaterally installed, moving-target cutoff, selectively lifted — confirmed operating on Melissa's phone. (2) Child preference relay. (3) Direct personal blocking (ROW-2639: 'No I did'). Selective enforcement confirmed five times. Returned in B-013 after 5-month gap (call timing gate, ROW-4785-4789). DND confirmed operational May 2024 (ROW-5264-5266, 20-month continuity). B-016: phone removal confirmed Jun 28 2024 (ROW-5802) — same 4-day window as Becky channel restriction and explicit permission framing. 24 cumulative signals through B-016.

signals.excerpt: She said she doesn't want to talk
It goes to voicemail after 8:30 because you've been told not to call after 8:30
I set it long time ago to do not disturb at 8:30 so you couldn't just keep calling after I tell you not to
She sent you to voicemail She said she doesn't want to talk
She's sending you to voicemail not me
You literally had all day to call but waited past 8:30
I've told you long ago that no calls after 8:30
I have her call you back those nights that you don't call on time if it's not interfering with school schedule
I changed the cutoff time because you couldn't respect boundaries / I'm just no longer making exceptions every night for you
Cause it's 8:30
I don't need a text. Just call her before 8:30.
She is not calling you this late even if she is awake.
If you called at 8:30 my do not disturb is on. I'll turn it off since she can sleep in and you can call back
She sent you to voicemail
No I did. We were in the middle of playing Roblox
So my phone automatically sends you to vm
What time did you call? Cause it automatically goes to that but I turned it off like an hour ago
Yeah. It's past 8:30
Yeah. Do not disturb kicks on at 8:30
It's past time / You've been told to call before 8:30 / You need to respect boundaries
Cause you treat the cut off time as the target time to call
Don't call yet / She's on the phone with Greyson [Eli had already called; Olivia still on competing call 17 min later]
She said you didn't call / I did it just went straight to 'not answering' / Past 8:30 then [DND operational 20 months after first documentation]
She doesn't have her phone [direct contact channel eliminated; same 4-day window as Becky channel restriction and permission framing]
She doesn't have her phone [Olivia's direct contact channel down; routine phone removal, direct statement]

Yeah. That's why I answered and told her that otherwise cutoff is 9:30 [ROW-7582 Melissa sarcastic confirmation: 'So she should call at 9:29 like you did all the time'; cutoff shifted from 8:30 to 9:30 PM, enforcement structure unchanged; 21+ month DND continuity confirmed]

I saw you come across her phone and she immediately flipped you to voicemail [clearest third-party observational instance of deliberate call blocking in dataset; Melissa present and witnesses Olivia affirmatively sending Eli to voicemail; resumes arc after 17-month quiet streak (B-021–B-030); occurs Apr 1, two days before Signal 64 enforcement (Apr 3) when Eli's direct text to Olivia is intercepted]

selective_enforcement_events: ROW-1186 (B-004): 'I'll take the do not disturb off if you wanna call her in 5 minutes'
ROW-2550 (B-007): 'If you called at 8:30 my do not disturb is on. I'll turn it off since she can sleep in'
ROW-2899 (B-008): 'I don't care what time you call this week or next'
ROW-3006 (B-009): 'I told you last week that there's no curfew cause I'm on vacation'
ROW-3009 (B-009): DND was active when Eli called; Melissa confirms she manually disabled it an hour prior

active_arcs.title: Melissa as Required Intermediary for Eli-Olivia Communication
active_arcs.description: Melissa positions herself as the exclusive intermediary — objecting to direct Eli-Olivia scheduling conversations, systematically relaying preferences that close out contact opportunities, conditioning visits on compliance, controlling bidirectional communication initiation, and curating the content of Olivia-Eli conversations. Five explicit confirmations of the morning call architecture: ROW-3151, ROW-3353-3356, ROW-4368-4369, ROW-4383-4385, ROW-7478. ROW-4383-4385 most comprehensive: 'She normally only calls because I ask if she wants to or I just dial you.' B-019 ROW-7478 adds analytically significant past-tense shift: 'I used to ask her if she wanted to call you in the mornings' — implies prompting practice has ceased, which would structurally reduce Olivia-initiated calls. Required-intermediary norm now internalized by Eli (ROW-7206: 'I know better than to make plans with her and not you'; ROW-12439: 'I made it clear that you have to be ok with it' — Eli pre-enforces the rule with Olivia herself). CRITICAL DEVELOPMENT (B-022, Feb 14): Olivia herself now enforces the required-intermediary rule — when Eli asked Olivia about Valentine's Day pickup, she redirected him to seek Melissa's approval: 'She said ask you' (ROW-9096). Normalization trajectory complete: norm has migrated from Melissa's external enforcement → Eli's internalized compliance → Olivia's behavioral implementation. Content curation dimension (B-018 ROW-6820): 'I also give her things to tell you about. I remind her of some of the things she's told me.' Three-dimensional required-intermediary model: (1) access/call initiation control, (2) content curation/scripting, (3) conflict-relay mechanism. Homework-conditional-access sub-pattern: 4 instances (Jan 18 – Mar 20, 2024). Contact-conflict relay sub-pattern: 7 instances (Apr 4, Apr 8, May 1 2024 — B-014; Jul 2 — B-016; Jul 16 — B-017; Aug 27 — B-018; Dec 21 — B-021). Seven explicit required-intermediary enforcement instances: ROW-299, ROW-416, ROW-5797-5801, ROW-7214-7215, ROW-8685, ROW-9098-9099. Required-intermediary domain extended to affective/grievance communication (B-023 ROW-9131/9141-9143): Melissa asserts Olivia routinely directs concerns through Melissa to Eli because she is 'afraid' to talk to Eli directly; Eli explicitly rejects extension. Contact-relay accusation sub-pattern: 8 instances (Apr 4, Apr 8, May 1 2024 — B-014; Jul 2 — B-016; Jul 16 — B-017; Aug 27 — B-018; Dec 21 — B-021; Apr 13 2025 — B-024). Required-intermediary domain extended to therapeutic/counseling communication (B-025 ROW-9660, May 21): Melissa relays Olivia's counseling session experience through herself as required conduit; CA-RF02-02 challenge: Melissa claims Olivia wanted to share the session with Eli but directed Melissa to make the call; when Eli calls Olivia directly (ROW-9661), Olivia says she does not want to discuss it — divergence between relay claim and direct child response. Required-intermediary domain extended to safety/harm-reporting communication (B-026 ROW-9743, Jun 8): Melissa asserts Olivia directed her to relay safety concerns about feeling unsafe at Eli's house ('She told me she wanted me to talk to you about it'); CA-RF02-02 applicable with narrative-priming element: ROW-9739 reveals Melissa's triggering question was 'I asked if you were just going to threaten her' — leading question precedes relay, showing clearest documentary instance of possible narrative shaping before delivery as Olivia's organic safety concern. Required-intermediary pattern now documented in scheduling, affective, therapeutic, safety, and visit-access control communication domains. 60 cumulative signals through B-030. Homework-conditional-access sub-pattern resumed after 18-month gap (Signal 55, Sep 17 2025). Visit-refusal cascade sub-pattern now dominant — six consecutive months (B-028 Aug-Sep, B-029 Oct, B-030 Nov-Dec 2025). Most extreme relay claim: Signal 58 ROW-14773 'She said she doesn't want to spend the night with you for the next 5 years.' First physical visit-refusal-at-threshold documented (Signal 58, ROW-8424 Nov 8 2025). Structural arc evolution: Melissa has now articulated explicit broker-gating model — relays Olivia's objections, disclaims responsibility for non-visits, and conditions visit restoration on Eli remedying her relayed complaints (Signal 59-60). B-031 (Jan–Apr 2026, 4 new signals): arc reaches 64 cumulative signals. Signal 61 (Jan 10, Sev 2): ninth explicit enforcement — required-intermediary rule extended to daily behavior reporting (hygiene/conduct); Melissa reformulates even direct parent-child contact as requiring request through her phone. Signal 62 (Feb 15-16, Sev 2): indefinite weekend-pause relay — post-Valentine's incident triggers highest scope claim in arc: Olivia reported to want to 'pause weekends for awhile'; physical threshold refusal (ROW-8576, Feb 27). Signal 63 (Mar 9, Sev 1): 'traumatized' relay language — highest rhetorical weight in arc; Melissa positions herself as readiness evaluator for when Olivia can resume visits. Signal 64 (Apr 2-3, Sev 1): tenth explicit enforcement during reconciliation attempt — Eli's direct contact with Olivia to repair relationship intercepted and rerouted. Arc ends with intermediary structure intact and Olivia ultimately attending Eli's April weekend (ROW-15554) through Melissa-mediated brokering.

signals.excerpt: I've told you this before but you keep making plans with her
you keep making plans to pick her up with her and not me
She said she doesn't want to call but she loves you too
She said she doesn't to call.
She said she doesn't want to call back
She said to tell you that she loves you but doesn't really want to talk right now
She said she doesn't want to call though.
She said she doesn't want to right now
She said she don't want to talk right now
She said she doesn't want to call back
She said she doesn't want to call back right now
She said she doesn't want to talk right now
She said she doesn't want to look at you cause you hurt her feelings / She doesn't want to Google meet you
I normally ask her if she wants to call you in the am. I'm the reason she calls you most days.
No she wants to swim
She said she doesn't want to call
That's why you are called most mornings. Because I ask.
Well never mind. She said she doesn't want to talk [reversed within seconds]
She said she doesn't want to talk today
She wants to know if she can just stay here and play with her friend
I'm not going to have you take her in the middle of the day and fuck up our plans / We can call you when we're back home
She needs to do homework though. So if you take her you have to do a half hour of iready. Otherwise she can't go
It's usually me asking her if she wants to call you. I was busy and didn't ask her yesterday.
She normally only calls because I ask if she wants to or I just dial you. I didn't ask her so she didn't call. Once in awhile she asks on her own.
I just told her you called and told her she should call you back but she said not right now
She has homework that needs done. You able to actually get some homework done? I don't want excuses as to why you couldn't do it
If not she can't go [third homework-conditional-access instance]
Olivia is upset. She said that you think your game is more important than her [Melissa as required interpreter of contact failures]
home by 9 with half of her homework done when she gets home 30 min ready and 6 units of Lexia need to be completed [fourth homework-conditional-access instance]
Ahe said 'what the fuck is he going on about? He hasn't called me' [Melissa relays Olivia denying Eli called]
Your daughter is upset that you hung up on her / She said you hung up [Eli disputes — says he didn't answer]
She was mad because you hung up on her / She said she don't care [child refusal to call back relayed as contact closure]
If you want to talk to Olivia tonight you will need to call my phone / She's still cranky and said she didn't care [device removal routes contact through Melissa]
Don't be calling her unless you really need to [Becky/childcare provider channel restricted immediately after number provided]
Should've answered the phone last night asshole then we could have made plans if you wanted to see her / If you want to see her you're supposed to ask anyhow
You got her crying [fourth contact-conflict relay event]
She was crying earlier when I tried to call you [fifth contact-conflict relay; distress in context of failed contact attempt]

I've told her to have conversations and I also give her things to tell you about. I remind her of some of the things she's told me [explicit admission: Melissa scripts/curates content of Olivia-Eli calls; new sub-pattern: content mediation beyond access mediation]
Did you yell at her or something? / She called me crying from the bus [sixth contact-conflict relay; accusation framing before cause verified; crying was separation-related not Eli-caused]

I tell you that you need to make these plans the night before and instead you made them with her [explicit reassertion that scheduling must route through Melissa not Olivia; Eli acknowledges rule ROW-7206: 'I know better than to make plans with her and not you']

I used to ask her if she wanted to call you in the mornings to keep communication going [fifth explicit bidirectional control confirmation; past tense 'used to' implies practice has ceased — would structurally reduce Olivia-initiated calls]
She asked me to call you [Olivia's desire to contact Eli routes through Melissa as intermediary rather than as direct call from child; also appears as ROW-12423 in MMS dataset]

Sounds like I have to be. Stop talking to her about it before you talk to me [Eli asked Olivia about pickup before clearing with Melissa; sixth explicit enforcement of required-intermediary rule; Eli confirms internalization ROW-12439: 'I made it clear that you have to be ok with it'; also appears as ROW-12437 in MMS dataset]

You know she keeps wanting to talk to you and you keep hanging up to do other things. / That's not nice [Melissa narrates Olivia's frustrated contact desire while attributing deficit to Eli; Dec 27 context reveals Melissa was calling on Olivia's behalf for Meta gameplay, not Olivia initiating; supporting Dec 18 instance ROW-8718-8719: sick-child relay]

She said sure for tomorrow but you better do something fun [ROW-8907-8908: 'Idk / I'll ask her when she wakes up' — Olivia's visit willingness mediated through Melissa's consultation; approval attached to behavioral condition]

She sais she doesn't want to talk on the phone to you today because you upset her and she wants me to tell you to leave her alone tonight [Olivia's phone access decision transmitted through Melissa as required intermediary; deployed after visit cancellation and during sustained RF-04 escalation cascade; also appears as ROW-12741 in MMS dataset]

She shouldn't have to tell you to ask me / You should be doing that before ask her [seventh explicit required-intermediary enforcement; MOST SIGNIFICANT: Olivia herself redirected Eli to seek Melissa's approval when asked about Valentine's Day pickup — ROW-9096: 'She said ask you'; normalization confirmed at child level; also appears as ROW-12925-12926 in MMS dataset]

Her being nervous to talk to you isn't new. We've had conversations about that before where I told you she wanted me to call and talk to you about something because she was afraid to talk to you [ROW-9131] / She asked me to talk to you. That's what you aren't understanding. / She didn't feel comfortable talking to you about it. She asked me to. / That's why I'm involved [ROW-9141-9143] [Melissa extends required-intermediary claim into affective/grievance communication domain; Eli explicitly rejects extension ROW-9133/9137/9144 — first on-record rejection of intermediary claim, contrasting with his full internalization of scheduling intermediary norm]

She's in the meta / She said you took so long that now she's on the vr [Melissa narrates Olivia's behavioral response to Eli's <10 min bathroom break; temporal accusation framing ('you took so long'); softest contact-relay accusation instance in arc — no crying/upset, child moved to VR after brief wait; also appears as ROW-13344-13346 in MMS dataset]

I asked if she wanted to call you to tell you about the call and she said yes and told me to call [required-intermediary operating in therapeutic/counseling domain; Melissa positions herself as conduit for communicating Olivia's counseling experience to Eli — 'she told me to call'; CA-RF02-02 challenge: Eli bypasses relay and calls Olivia directly (ROW-9661), Olivia says she does not want to discuss the counseling session — diverges from Melissa's relay claim that Olivia wanted to share; also appears as ROW-13591 in MMS dataset]

She told me she wanted me to talk to you about it which is what I'm doing [safety-domain relay — Melissa positions herself as conduit for Olivia's reported feeling of being unsafe at Eli's; CA-RF02-02 applicable with narrative-priming element: ROW-9739 reveals Melissa's triggering question was 'I asked if you were just going to threaten her' — leading question precedes relay; required-intermediary pattern now in scheduling, affective, therapeutic, and safety domains; also appears as ROW-13705 in MMS dataset]

She says she's not going [visit-access domain: Melissa relays Olivia's refusal to attend scheduled visit with Eli; no child-direct contact attempt documented; first signal in Aug 26-Sep 5 six-day visit-refusal cascade]

That's why I helped facilitate a conversation between you two about how she feels since she is afraid to talk to you about things [first explicit first-person self-characterization of the required-intermediary role in dataset; Melissa articulates facilitator identity as protective necessity; parallels ROW-14097 Aug 14 counselor-reported fear claim]

Olivia said she doesn't want to go to your house tomorrow night / She said she's not going / She doesn't want to go [multi-day visit-refusal relay cascade; three days of sequential relay assertions; no child-direct contact mechanism available to Eli; parenting time access fully mediated through Melissa's preference relay]

If you can't follow thru on homework she won't be able to go there during the week anymore [homework-conditional-access 5th instance; 18-month gap since prior cluster B-012/B-013; Melissa conditions weekday parenting time access on homework compliance]

Olivia was begging me not to tell you cause she's so afraid of you / So I need you to not be yelling at her for telling me [Sev 2: three-element intermediary enforcement — (1) Olivia routes disclosures through Melissa due to fear of Eli, (2) Melissa relays, (3) Eli instructed not to punish Olivia for using Melissa-channel]

Olivia says she doesn't want to spend the weekend with you / [cascade] / Olivia said don't bother coming [third consecutive visit-refusal cascade across B-028 and B-029; Eli's scheduled parenting weekend cancelled entirely through relay mechanism]

She says she doesn't want to go with you this weekend anyways / She said she doesn't want to spend the night with you for the next 5 years / Call her cause she refuses to come out. It was a mess to get her out of the car even [Sev 2: extreme 5-year duration relay claim; first physical visit-refusal-at-threshold documented]

Also Olivia says she doesn't want to stay at your house this weekend / She said she's not coming out tomorrow / Not my fault she doesn't want to go. You need to fix the things she says she's upset about so she wants to go [Melissa disclaims relay responsibility while conditioning visit restoration on Eli's remediation of relayed complaints]

Could you please talk to her about things she's voiced concerns about? / That's why I'm telling you to talk to her so that maybe she will go / The biggest reason she gives is Milo always fighting with her. Any ideas of how that can be remedied would help her [Melissa explicitly positions herself as broker-intermediary conditioning Eli's parenting time access on remediation of relayed grievances]

You should talk to me about these things instead of her [ROW-14978] / If you really wanted to talk to her you could call my phone and ask to talk to her [ROW-14984] [ninth explicit enforcement; Eli contacted Olivia directly about hygiene/behavior concerns; Melissa intercepts and extends required-intermediary rule into daily behavior reporting domain; ROW-14983: 'I think a lot of the behavior problems while she's at your house stems from her not wanting to be there. She has refused to go there every weekend' — sweeping characterization co-deployed with enforcement]

Olivia called me and said she is scared / She said she doesn't want to go to your house on weekends at all / She pretty upset and said she would like to pause weekends for awhile, not forever cause she wants to see her sister / I'm just telling you what she said [post-Valentine's incident relay; indefinite weekend-pause claim — highest scope assertion in arc; physical threshold refusal ROW-8576 Feb 27 'You can come but she says she's not coming out'; Melissa as broker-disclaiming relay channel; ROW-15153-15155 Feb 28 'I talked her into letting me call you in the car so you could apologize. You sent us to voicemail']

Olivia still says she doesn't want to go / She said you traumatized her and she's still not ready to go back to your house yet / I'm trying to help her work through it ['traumatized' is new language in arc — highest rhetorical weight applied to a relay claim in dataset; Melissa positions herself as therapeutic intermediary and readiness evaluator; ROW-15482 Mar 23 'Olivia still says she's not going this weekend'; ROW-15486 Mar 25 'she says she doesn't want to go. I'd suggest calling and talking to her' — continued weekend-pause relay]

Olivia told you last Friday that she would be more comfortable coming over on the weekends and being around you again if you'd pick her up during the week for a few hours [ROW-15510] / You should be reaching out to me about it not her [ROW-15513] [tenth explicit enforcement; Eli texted Olivia directly in reconciliation attempt; Melissa intercepts and reasserts intermediary rule; even Eli's direct attempts to repair relationship must route through Melissa channel; ROW-15554 Apr 9 'She says she will go to your house this weekend though' — Olivia ultimately attended; arc ends with intermediary structure intact]

active_arcs.title: Blanket 'Not to Your House' Location Restriction
active_arcs.description: Melissa restricts Olivia from being taken to Eli's home across multiple contexts and time periods. B-004: medically mitigated (cat allergy, pre-surgery). B-006: post-surgery, no mitigation, Melissa asserts indefinite authority ('As many times as I need'). B-008: air quality justification (Canadian wildfire smoke) — mitigated. B-012: December 26 2023, no justification — triggers ACTIVE promotion. 4 cumulative signals through B-012. Arc naturally suspended B-014+: cat Tahko died Apr 1 2024 (original allergy rationale moot); both parties agreed to pause house visits pending cleaning. No new restriction events B-014–B-016. Bug inquiry (B-016, ROW-5831-5832) resolved cooperatively.

signals.excerpt: And not your house
And not to your house [+ 'As many times as I need']
can't be outside though or at your house. The air quality alert was extended through tomorrow
She can't go to your house

active_arcs.title: Out-of-State Relocation Intent
active_arcs.description: Melissa has raised relocation across four signals spanning 18 months: (1) B-013, Feb 21 2024: 'You still good if we move to NC? I may be able to get into a job over there fairly easily' — direct inquiry with employment rationale, highest analytical weight; (2) B-015, May 26 2024 (Outer Banks vacation): 'This is exactly why I want to live out here' — vacation sentiment, attenuated; (3) B-017, Jul 16 2024 (return from Canada vacation): 'She says she is sad cause she's going to miss Canada. Wants to move to Canada or North Carolina' — child preference relay, most attenuated; promoted CANDIDATE→WATCHING at B-017 (third reference reached). (4) B-028, Aug 14 2025: 'Also I've been looking at houses and may move soon' — first action-step signal: present-tense active house-searching with near-term timeline. Satisfies promotion criterion: housing-inquiry action step. Embedded in Aug 14 escalation exchange alongside full custody threat (ROW-14154-14155) and 'She's better off with me full time' (ROW-14080) — relocation disclosure co-occurs with custody elimination framing. No court notification of relocation intent documented in dataset. Promoted WATCHING→ACTIVE at B-028.

signals.excerpt: You still good if we move to NC? I may be able to get into a job over there fairly easily
We don't want to go home. This is exactly why I want to live out here [from Outer Banks vacation, Corolla NC]
She says she is sad cause she's going to miss Canada. Wants to move to Canada or North Carolina [child preference relayed by Melissa on return from Canada vacation]

Also I've been looking at houses and may move soon [first action-step signal: present-tense active house-searching with near-term timeline; embedded in Aug 14 escalation alongside full custody threat ROW-14154-14155 and 'She's better off with me full time' ROW-14080; satisfies housing-inquiry promotion criterion; no court notification documented]

watching_arcs.title: Systematic Abby Exclusion from Olivia's Life
watching_arcs.description: Melissa systematically excludes Eli's partner Abby from Olivia's access contexts. Signal 1 (B-007): Visit access conditioned on Abby's absence. Signal 2 (B-010): School pickup authorization removed unilaterally. No new signals B-011 through B-016. 2 signals.

signals.excerpt: She said that she doesn't want to go if Abby is there / if Abby's in the car she won't have to go
I deleted her off of pickup / Abby. Lol

watching_arcs.promotion_threshold: ACTIVE threshold: 3 signals or escalation to Sev 2 visit denial without preference relay intermediation
watching_arcs.counter_signal_note: B-017 (Jul–Aug 2024): Three explicit Abby-inclusive invitations (ROW-6117-6118, ROW-6127-6128, ROW-6501) — most significant cooperative Abby engagement in dataset. No new restriction signals since B-010 (Sep 2023). Arc remains WATCHING pending sustained pattern assessment.

candidate_arcs.title: Melissa Overriding Olivia's Stated Preference for Eli Contact
candidate_arcs.description: Melissa denies Eli access while Olivia herself wants his presence. Single signal (B-002, Halloween 2022). No new signals through B-017.

signals.excerpt: We don't need you / She wanted you to go. I didn't need you to go with her.
